content.full_text:
City of Alhambra
(818)570-5091

February 15, 1991

John Wallace, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Campaign Contribution from One "local" Primarly
Formed Committee to Another "local" Primarly
Formed Committee

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This letter is being written as per your request.

FACTS:

1. On April 10, 1990, a Special Municipal Election was
held in Alhambra on a City measure seeking voter
approval to repeal an ordinance which prohibited
the sale of any hospital owned or leased by the
City without obtaining voter approval of such a
sale.

2. Committee No. 1 was formed by a group of citizens
to support the passage of this measure.

3. Committee No. 1 presently has approximately $800 in
funds and now wishes to dispose of these funds and
terminate.

3. The Alhambra City Council is now in the process of
calling a Special Municipal Election to be held on
June 4, 1991 seeking voter approval of an advisory
measure for the construction a new police facility.

4. Committee No. 2 has been formed by a group of
citizens to support the passage of this advisory
measure.

QUESTION:

Can Committee No. 1 contribute its surplus funds to
Committee No. 2?

The Government Code and FPPC Regulations are silent
with respect to contributions from one Primarly
Formed Committee to another Primarly Formed
Committee, both of which were formed to support or
oppose a city measure.

John Wallace, Esq.
February 15, 1991
Page 2


RESPONSE:

     This will confirm your verbal response to me this
morning that given the above set of circumstances
Committee No. 1 legally may contribute funds to
Committee No. 2.

     If my understanding of your response is incorrect,
please notify me at once by telephone in order that I
may so notify the two committees.

Thank you for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

FRANCES A. MOORE, CMC
CITY CLERK

cc:  Mr. Mark Paulson, Treasurer
     Help our City

     Mr. Lawrence G. Seligman, Treasurer
     Alhambra Citizens Against Crime


If you could provide a higher contrast version of this document or clarify which specific text you need transcribed, I would be happy to help.
